door_a.caption:
Comment “Photo” and I’ll send you all the prompts 👇

What happens when you ask ChatGPT to find every photo of you on the internet?

I tried this and it found me in photos I didn’t even know existed.

Prompt 1:
Upload a selfie and say: “Analyze this photo and list out every specific feature that makes me recognizable so you can identify me anywhere online.”

ChatGPT builds a full description of exactly what you look like.

Here’s the trick:

Turn on Agent Mode and say: “Using my selfie and everything you know about me, go reverse image search my face across the top tools and find every single place I appear on the internet and give me a link to each one.”

ChatGPT opens its own browser, runs your face through every reverse image search, and comes back with a link to every place you show up.

The results were terrifying.

Bonus (if you value your privacy):
“Now write me custom removal requests for each of these sites to get my photos taken down.”

ChatGPT writes custom takedown requests for every single photo.

Be safe out there.

#chatgpt #ai #privacy #chatgptprompts #aitools

Comment “Human” and I’ll send you the full guide 👇

What happens when you ask ChatGPT to humanize your text so it passes every AI detector?

I tried this and my text came back as 0% AI generated.

Here’s how:

Say: “Go to Wikipedia and look up the page on signs of AI writing and list out every pattern that gives away AI text.”
ChatGPT pulls every tell like em dashes, filler phrases, and that robotic tone.

Then say: “Turn all these patterns into a strict set of writing rules you should never break, and give me a permanent instruction prompt that makes everything you write sound completely human and pass AI detectors.”
Here’s the trick:

Copy that, go to Settings → Personalization, and paste it into your custom instructions.

Now ChatGPT saves it into its permanent memory so everything you write from now on comes back as 0% AI detected.

#chatgpt #ai #chatgptprompts #aihumanizer #chatgpttips

Comment “Identity” and I’ll send you all the prompts 👇

What happens when you ask Claude to erase your entire digital footprint off the internet?

I tried this and it wiped my identity off hundreds of websites while I slept.

Prompt 1:
“Search the internet for everything you can find about me. List every data broker and people search site exposing my personal info, and give me the exact opt-out link for each one.”

The results were terrifying. Claude found sites I’ve never heard of selling my address and phone number.

Prompt 2:
“Write me the exact removal request to send to each one.”

Claude writes custom removal requests for every single site.

Here’s the trick:

Go to Customize → Connectors → Browse Connectors, search Google Chrome, and install it.

Prompt 3:
“Now use Chrome to go to every one of those sites and submit my removal request customized to each one.”

Claude opens its own browser and submits every request while you do nothing.

Prompt 4:
“Now recheck every site every week and resubmit any that get ignored until I’m completely gone.”

Claude builds a scheduled task that runs every week until your identity is wiped for good.

Be safe out there.

#ai #claude #privacy #databrokers #aitools

Comment “Resume” and I’ll send you the full guide 👇

What happens when you ask ChatGPT to rewrite your resume so it’s basically un-rejectable?

I tried this and landed 6 job interviews in 24 hours.

Prompt 1:
Upload your resume and job description and say: “Act as a senior recruiter for this exact company. Analyze my resume against this job description and give me a match score out of 100, the top 5 keywords I’m missing, and 3 red flags a hiring manager would spot in under 10 seconds.”

Prompt 2:
“Rewrite my experience section to naturally include those keywords and remove every red flag using the Google XYZ formula: Accomplish X as measured by Y by doing Z.”

Prompt 3 (most important):
“Now act as an applicant tracking system. Scan my new resume and tell me exactly which sections a bot would fail to read, then rewrite them so they pass every filter.”

Here’s the coolest part:

Turn on Agent Mode and say: “Now go find every job matching this resume posted in the last 7 days and apply to the top 50 for me, tailoring each application to the job description.”

ChatGPT applies to every single one while you do absolutely nothing.

#chatgpt #ai #resume #jobsearch #career

Claude lies to you 17% of the time.

Here’s one prompt that forces it to always tell the truth.

It’s called the Truth Prompt, developed by researchers at MIT to stop Claude from guessing, filling in gaps, and confidently telling you things that aren’t even true.

Here’s how to install it:

Go to Claude and click your name in the bottom left
Go to Settings → Instructions for Claude
Paste it in and hit Save
Now Claude will:
• Verify and check its logic, facts, completeness, and bias before every answer
• Reflect on its own answer
• Retry the output if the truth confidence meter is below 0.8

One paste. Saves forever.

Now Claude stops lying and starts telling you the truth.

Comment “Expert” and I’ll send you the full prompting framework 👇

Most people use Claude wrong and get generic answers back.

Here’s how to go from beginner to expert in 4 easy steps:

Give it the outcome
If Claude knows the end goal, it works backwards to give you the best possible answer. Don’t just give it a task, tell it exactly where you’re trying to end up.

Give it the task
Be specific. “Find me 50 leads that are ideal customers for my business” beats “help me get customers” every single time.

Give it context
This is the one everyone skips. Upload your docs, your brand voice, and anything that explains your situation. The more context you give it, the better your answer.

Give it the format
Tell Claude exactly how you want it to respond—a spreadsheet, a bulleted list, or even “explain it like I’m in seventh grade.”

Do these 4 things and your Claude answers get 200% better.

#ai #claude #claudeprompts #aitools #productivity

Most people use Claude wrong and get generic answers back.

Here’s how to go from beginner to expert in 4 easy steps:

Give it the outcome
If Claude knows the end goal, it works backwards to give you the best possible answer. Don’t just give it a task, tell it exactly where you’re trying to end up.

Give it the task
Be specific. “Find me 50 leads that are ideal customers for my business” beats “help me get customers” every single time.

Give it context
This is the one everyone skips. Upload your docs, your brand voice, and anything that explains your situation. The more context you give it, the better your answer.

Give it the format
Tell Claude exactly how you want it to respond—a spreadsheet, a bulleted list, or even “explain it like I’m in seventh grade.”

Do these 4 things and your Claude answers get 200% better.
